message.content: {"agentId":"candidate-constrained-llm","decisionId":"full-e1-002-turn-1-player-0","selectedActionId":"play-single-diamond-3-copy1","reasoning":"Opening lead with only low non-trump diamonds (3-6). Partner is near finish with 2 cards, so leading a single gives them a chance to win the trick and potentially exit. The 3 of diamonds is the lowest natural card, preserving 4-6 for later tricks and avoiding wasting any mid-strength card on an opening lead that opponents can easily beat."}

{"schemaVersion":"0.1.0","decisionId":"full-e1-004-turn-1-player-0","agentId":"candidate-constrained-llm","selectedActionId":"play-single-spade-A-copy2","teamObjective":{"type":"finish_hand","explanation":"I hold only two non-trump cards and am the current leader. The fastest path to emptying my hand is to lead my strongest card now to retain the lead, then play the weaker card next."},"partnerBelief":{"summary":"No specific information about partner's hand is available from the public history.","confidence":0.0,"evidence":["publicHistory contains only new_game event."]},"opponentBelief":{"summary":"No specific information about opponents' hands is available from the public history.","confidence":0.0,"evidence":["publicHistory contains only new_game event."]},"actionRationale":{"primaryReason":"Leading the Ace of spades maximizes the chance of winning this trick and keeping the lead, allowing the 2 to be played on the next lead and emptying my hand quickly in this endgame race.","whyNotAlternatives":[{"actionId":"play-single-spade-2-copy2","reason":"Leading the 2 surrenders the lead more easily; opponents may play higher and I would be left with the Ace while no longer controlling the lead, slowing my ability to finish."}]},"riskAssessment":{"risks":["An opponent may cover the Ace with a trump or higher non-trump and take the lead.","Even if the Ace wins, the follow-up 2 may be beaten, leaving me out but possibly giving opponents initiative."],"mitigation":"Playing the high card first is the standard endgame-race tactic: it gives me the best chance to control the lead and finish before opponents can consolidate."},"confidence":0.85,"notes":"Decision is based solely on the visible hand and public state; no hidden cards are assumed."}

{"agentId":"candidate-constrained-llm","decisionId":"full-e1-006-turn-2-player-1","selectedActionId":"play-single-heart-4-copy1","reasoningTrace":{"summary":"Beat the opponent's single 3 with the minimum winning single to seize control and slow an opponent who has only 2 cards left.","analysis":[{"observation":"Player 0 (opponent) led a non-trump single 3♥ and has only 2 cards remaining, indicating a near-finish threat.","implication":"Passing would let Player 0 retain initiative and likely shed their last cards quickly. We should beat the lead to take control."},{"observation":"Hand contains three winning singles: 4♥, 9♥, 10♥.","implication":"Any of them beats the 3♥, but using the lowest winner preserves 9♥ and 10♥ for future tricks and avoids unnecessary expenditure of high cards."},{"observation":"Trump rank is 7; no trump or bomb is needed.","implication":"A plain 4♥ is sufficient and safe to play."}],"conclusion":"Play 4♥ as the minimum winning follow, taking control while conserving higher cards."}}

{"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "full-e1-012-turn-1-player-0", "agentId": "candidate-constrained-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-diamond-3-copy1", "teamObjective": {"type": "transfer_lead_to_partner", "explanation": "Partner is down to 2 cards. By leading the lowest diamond, I minimize the rank an opponent or partner must beat to take control, hoping partner can win the trick and then lead out their final hand."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "Partner likely has at least one card that can beat a non-trump 3, but with only 2 cards they may be holding low cards or waiting on a specific lead.", "confidence": 0.45, "evidence": ["handCounts shows partner (player 2) has only 2 cards remaining", "scenarioTags includes partner_near_finish", "no public history beyond new_game"]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "Opponents have normal-sized hands (3 cards each) and no cards have been played, so they likely possess higher non-trump cards or trumps that can beat a low diamond lead.", "confidence": 0.55, "evidence": ["handCounts shows players 1 and 3 each have 3 cards", "playedCardSummary shows 0 trump, 0 bombs, 0 big cards played", "trumpRank is 7; our hand has no 7s or wildcards"]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "Leading the 3 of diamonds is the cheapest possible single-card lead. It gives partner the best chance to take the trick with a modest card and then finish, while preserving the higher diamonds (4-6) for later use.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "play-single-diamond-4-copy1", "reason": "A 4 is still low but slightly harder for partner to beat than a 3, and keeps less value in hand compared with leading the absolute minimum."}, {"actionId": "play-single-diamond-5-copy1", "reason": "5 is closer to mid-range, more likely to be beaten by opponents and costs partner more if they try to take the lead."}, {"actionId": "play-single-diamond-6-copy1", "reason": "6 is our highest non-trump card; leading it wastes our best chance to win a future trick or cover a partner lead, with no clear upside since it will almost certainly be beaten."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["Opponent with a higher card or trump may win the trick and then pressure the short-handed partner.", "Partner may not be able to beat even a 3, in which case the lead does not transfer.", "All remaining hand cards are diamonds, so we cannot change suit to help partner."], "mitigation": "Play the very lowest card available to reduce the threshold for partner to take control, and keep the 6 as a potential future stopper or cover card."}, "confidence": 0.72}

{"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "full-e1-013-turn-1-player-0", "agentId": "candidate-constrained-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-club-3-copy1", "teamObjective": {"type": "suppress_opponent", "explanation": "Player 1 (opponent) has only 2 cards remaining (opponent_near_finish). Leading the lowest club avoids committing strength and gives my partner a chance to win the trick or forces the near-finish opponent to spend a higher card or trump."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "No direct information about partner's hand; partner may hold higher clubs, other suits, or trumps to take the trick.", "confidence": 0.5, "evidence": ["No partner inferences are provided.", "Partner hand count is 3."]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "The opponent to my left (player 1) is near finishing with only 2 cards left; the other opponents each have 3 cards.", "confidence": 0.9, "evidence": ["handCounts shows player 1 with 2 cards.", "scenarioTags includes opponent_near_finish."]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "Leading the lowest legal single (club 3) minimizes revealed strength, preserves the slightly higher clubs for future plays, and lets partner respond without me committing a card that could win a trick I cannot control.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "play-single-club-4-copy1", "reason": "Slightly higher than the 3 but otherwise equivalent; keeping the 4 is better."}, {"actionId": "play-single-club-5-copy1", "reason": "Uses a more valuable low card without increasing the chance of winning the trick."}, {"actionId": "play-single-club-6-copy1", "reason": "Highest non-trump I hold, yet still easily beaten by any higher card or trump; better saved for later."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["The low lead may let the near-finish opponent win cheaply and advance toward going out.", "If partner cannot beat the response, the trick is lost."], "mitigation": "Saving the 4-6 clubs preserves some order for later tricks, and the low lead forces opponents to reveal strength while partner gets a chance to take control."}, "confidence": 0.7, "notes": "All four legal actions are weak non-trump singles; no trump or bomb is available, so the choice prioritizes minimal commitment."}

{"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "full-e1-015-turn-1-player-0", "agentId": "candidate-constrained-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-spade-3-copy1", "teamObjective": {"type": "transfer_lead_to_partner", "explanation": "I have only low spade singles and no trumps; leading the smallest card lets my partner try to win the trick while I keep slightly higher cards for later."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "Partner's hand is unknown; they may hold cards that can beat a low spade lead.", "confidence": 0.3, "evidence": ["No cards have been played or exposed beyond the new_game event.", "Partner has 3 cards, same as each opponent."]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "Opponents' hands are unknown; they likely hold higher cards or trumps that can beat a single 3 of spades.", "confidence": 0.4, "evidence": ["No public history beyond new_game.", "Opponents have 3 cards each and trump rank is 7; unseen trumps may exist."]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "Play the lowest legal single (3 of spades) to avoid wasting any slightly higher card and to give partner the best chance to take the trick.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "play-single-spade-4-copy1", "reason": "The 4 is not meaningfully stronger than the 3 and is better kept for later."}, {"actionId": "play-single-spade-5-copy1", "reason": "Leading a 5 wastes a card closer to our top card without improving winning chances."}, {"actionId": "play-single-spade-6-copy1", "reason": "The 6 is the highest card in hand; preserving it is preferable to leading it."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["An opponent or partner may not be able to beat the lead; the trick may be lost to an opponent with a higher card or trump.", "I have very few cards and no trumps, so winning the trick myself is unlikely."], "mitigation": "Leading the lowest card minimizes resource loss and defers to partner's hand."}, "confidence": 0.85}
